title: digitalis canariensis f. tomentosa
intro: Digitalis canariensis f. tomentosa, also known as the Canary Island Foxglove or Woolly Foxglove, is a variety of the Digitalis canariensis plant, a species of flowering plant in the foxglove family native to the Canary Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a cardiac glycoside, which helps to regulate heart rhythms and increase cardiac output. The health benefits of Digitalis canariensis f. tomentosa include its potential to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation, congestive heart failure, and hypertension, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Digitalis canariensis f. tomentosa can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
